content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                      322-6441

March 29, 1977

Alfred R. Angelino, Member
State Resource Conservation Commission
2430 Dunlap Avenue
Gilroy, California 95020

Dear Mr. Angelino:

In answer to your letter of February 17, 1977, in which you ask the
following:

1. Will there be any conflicts in the holding of or of the
   positions? If so, what are they and what limitations
   should I exercise?

2. How far can I go in talking with legislators from out-
   side of my own districts in such matters?

3. Would my functions require that I register for such
   purposes? If so, what is the procedure?

4. Is there any other concern not covered herein that is
   applicable?

The above-stated questions are asked in light of the fact that you
presently hold the following positions; District Director of the Loma
Prieta Resource Conservation District, Commissioner on the State Resource
Conservation Commission, Vice-President of the California Association of
Resource Conservation Districts (CARCD) an unincorporated association, and
Legislative Chairman of CARCD.

In answer to question #1, the only foreseeable conflict that could
occur would be as a result of your receiving $250 or more in expenses
during the course of a calendar year, and a decision which could materially
affect CARCD comes before you in your capacity as a State Resource Conser-
vation Commissioner.

Government Code Section 87100 states:

87100. Public Officials; State and Local. No public official
at any level of state or local government shall make, participate
in making or in any way attempt to use his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason

Mr. A.R. Angelino
page 2
March 29, 1977

to know he has a financial interest.

CARCD would thus be a source of income over $250 as defined in
Government Code Section 87103(c) which states:

87103. Financial Interest. An official has a financial interest
in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one
thousand dollars ($1,000)

(b) Any real property in which the public official has
a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

(c) Any source of income, other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars or more in value
received by or promised to the public official within
twelve months prior to the time when the decision is made;"

Government Code Section which defines Income includes, reimbursement
for expenses and per diem within the definition of income.

Government Code Section 82030 defines Income as:

Income. (a) "Income" means, except as provided in subsection (b),
income of any nature from any source, including but not limited
to any salary, wage, advance, payment, dividend, interest, rent,
capital gain, return of capital, gift, including any gift of
food or beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness,
discount in the price of anything of value unless the discount
is made available to members of the public without regard to
official status, rebate, reimbursement for expenses, per diem
or contribution to an insurance or pension program paid by any
person other than an employer...."

In the unlikely event that a conflict situation as described above
should occur you would have to disqualify yourself from participating in
the decision.

However, if CARCD an unincorporated association, qualified under
Government Code Section 82030(b) 2, as a bonafide, academic or charitable

Mr. A.R. Angelino
page three
March 29, 1977

institution, it would not be a source of income within the meaning
of the Political Reform Act and consequently; not a reason for
disclosure or disqualification.

Government Code Section 82030(b)(2) states:

(b) "Income"does not include:

(2) Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received
from a state or local government agency and reimbursement for
travel expenses and per diem received from a bonafide educational,
academic or charitable organization;

The definition of a lobbyist as defined in Government Code Section 82039
is:

82039. Lobbyist. "Lobbyist" means any person who is employed
or contracts for economic consideration, other than reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses, to communicate directly
or through his agents with any elective state official, agency
official or legislative official for the purpose of influencing
legislative or administrative action, if a substantial or
regular portion of the activities for which he receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing legislative or
administrative action. No person is a lobbyist by reason of
activities described in Section 86300.

Therefore in answer to question #3., it does not appear that you fall
within the Lobbyist definition by virtue of any of your described positions,
as such you would not have to register as a lobbyist.

In answer to your second question, it would thus appear based upon
the answer to question #3, that you would not be prevented from continuing
to contact the legislators outside your district as you presently do, nor
would such activities in any way change the result in question #3 above.

Finally, in answer to question #4, CARCD should be aware that IF it
does not fall within the exception in Government Section 82030(b)(2),
stated above, and it makes payments to influence legislative action of
$250.00 or more in value in any month, CARCD would be required to file a
statement under Government Code Section 86108(b).

Government Code Section 86108(b) states:

Periodic Reports; Employers and Others. Subject to the exceptions
in Section 86300, the following persons shall file statements
required by Section 86109:

(b) Any person who directly or indirectly makes payments to
influence legislative or administrative action of two hundred

Mr. A.R. Angelino
page four
March 29, 1977

fifty dollars ($250) or more in value in any month, unless
all of the payments are of the type described in Section 82045(c).

If I can be of further assistance, please call me at 916/322-6444.

Sincerely,

Alfredo Flores
Research Specialist
Conflicts of Interest Division

AF:mfa

2430 Dunlap Avenue
Altadena, Calif. 95020
February 17, 1977

Via Political n New Commission
100 K Street Hill
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Dr. Alfredo Flores

Dear Sir:

In regard to our telephone conversation of February 17th, I request any further info legality or clarification as to possible conflicts or limitations within the following areas:

I am presently Director of the Loan Priest Senorique Conservation Districts in Sutter County. I am a newly elected County Commissioner of the State Resource Conservation Commission. I am also serving as vice-president to the California Association of Resource Conservation Districts (CARD), a non-profit organization of member districts in the state.

There is no pay involved in any of the four positions, only travel and per diem expenses of the time actually involved.

I have been appointed legislative chairman of the CARD. As legislative chairman I would be the duty to monitor and support or oppose legislation affecting Resource Conservation Districts or to [illegible] district support through the association on [illegible] to support districts.

Our normal procedure in this is to request district directors to report to their own legislators as to the good or bad points of a particular bill or bills and how they would affect the people of the districts.

My questions are as follows:

1. Will there be any conflicts in the holding of or functions of the positions? If so, what are they and what limitations should I exercise?

2. How far can I go in talking with legislators from outside of my own district in such matters?

3. Would my functions require that I register for such purpose? If so, what is the procedure?

Fair Political Practices Commission - 2/17/77 - II

4. Is there any other concern not covered herein that is applicable?

Please advise.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Alfred R. Angelino, Member
State Resource Conservation
Commission

ATTN:
CC:  State Resource Conservation Commission
     CARGD
